whatWasImplemented: src/app/api/crawl/start/route.ts — checkCrawlQuota() helper: reads caller's plan + usage_counters, returns {allowed:false, used, limit} when used >= plan.monthly_crawl_page_limit, else {allowed:true}. Wired between the subscription gate and the existing-site guard — 402 with upgrade_url + no Firecrawl call when over quota. Starter fallback (500) when plan_id null.

src/lib/crawl/process.ts — after the atomic batch swap, processCrawlData reads current crawl_pages_used, adds uniquePages.size, and UPDATEs usage_counters. Cumulative within the period (M3F4 resets on invoice.paid). Skipped cleanly on zero-page crawls by the existing 'No valid content' guard — the increment only runs after the successful swap.
src/__tests__/crawl-quota.test.ts — 5 route tests: 402 at-limit, 402 over-limit, 200 under-limit, Starter fallback for null plan_id, existing-site 409 still fires when quota OK.
src/__tests__/crawl-process-quota.test.ts — 2 unit tests with mocked Supabase + OpenAI: seed 10, land 3 pages -> counter becomes 13; throw-before-increment when 0 valid pages.
discoveredIssues: Existing crawl-start.test.ts needed profile/plans/usage_counters mock additions to preserve the pre-existing 'returns 200' and 'calls Firecrawl' tests — fixed in this feature.
Existing crawl-process.test.ts needed user_id on the sites mock + usage_counters mock so downstream increment doesn't crash the pre-existing pipeline tests — fixed here.

The increment path does two round-trips (SELECT current, UPDATE new). An atomic UPDATE ... SET crawl_pages_used = crawl_pages_used + N would eliminate the race, but crawl completion is serialized per site at the webhook layer so the race window is narrow. Worth revisiting in M8 if audit finds drift.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 316 passed / 1 skipped / 44 files (+7 new across crawl-quota + crawl-process-quota, plus 2 fixture patches to keep pre-existing tests green).

manualChecks.action: processCrawlData with 3 unique pages, seed counter=10
